MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-02-2015 Set For Trial On Oct-17-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jul-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BRYAN MCCORMACK OF MCCORMACK AND ERLICH LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-296-8420 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. MATTHEW M. HAFFNER OF THE HAFFNER LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 1-805-641-9334 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CANUS CORPORATION BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Canus Corporation's motion for judgment on the pleadings on both claims alleged in the complaint of plaintiff Thomas Jones is denied as to both claims. While Mr. Jones has not alleged wrongful conduct directly committed by Canus, he has alleged that Canus and PG&E were in an agency relationship with each other which is sufficient to hold Canus liable for the alleged misconduct of PG&E. Mr. Jones has adequately alleged claims for wrongful termination in violation of public policy grounded in Labor Code 1102.5 and for violation of Labor Code 1102.5. (See CACI 2430 and 2730). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk
